content.cn_text: 第四部分 你将听到一个讲座的一部分，内容是关于
content.en_text: Part 4 You will hear part of a lecture about

content.cn_text: 一种早期摄影形式，它在英国和法国发展起来，被称为卡拉类型摄影。
content.en_text: an early form of photography that developed in Britain and France called Cala type photography.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间来查看问题31到40。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答问题31到40。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 大家早上好。
content.en_text: Morning everyone.

content.cn_text: 欢迎回到这个关于摄影发展系列讲座。
content.en_text: Welcome back to this series of lectures on the development of photography.

content.cn_text: 上周我们讨论了一种名为达盖尔照相法的摄影类型。
content.en_text: Last week we looked at a type of photography called daguerreotype.

content.cn_text: 第一种商业上成功的摄影工艺。
content.en_text: The first commercially successful photographic process.

content.cn_text: 今天我们将探讨它是如何逐渐被一种名为卡拉类型法（Cala type）的技术所取代的。
content.en_text: Today we're going to look at how this was gradually replaced by a technique called Cala type.

content.cn_text: 达盖尔照相法是一个重大突破，但它也存在一些问题。
content.en_text: daguerreotype was a major breakthrough but there were problems with it.

content.cn_text: 特别是由于图像是制作在铜板上，制作每张照片都非常昂贵。
content.en_text: In particular because the image was produced on a copper sheet it was expensive to create each photograph.

content.cn_text: 因此，在19世纪40年代，一位名叫威廉·塔尔博特的人开始寻找一种替代方法。
content.en_text: As a result during the 1840s a man named William Talbot began looking for an alternative approach.

content.cn_text: 塔尔博特最终成为一位有影响力的人物，但他总是说自己不是专业的摄影师。
content.en_text: Talbot eventually became an influential figure but he always said he was not a professional photographer.

content.cn_text: 事实上，他接受过艺术家培训。他最大的兴趣是绘画。
content.en_text: In fact he trained as an artist. His greatest interest was painting.

content.cn_text: 他只是将摄影作为次要兴趣涉足。
content.en_text: And he only became involved with photography as a secondary interest.

content.cn_text: 塔尔博特花费多年试图解决与达盖尔照相法相关的问题。
content.en_text: Talbot spent many years trying to solve the problems associated with daguerreotype photography.

content.cn_text: 他尝试了多种不同的方法，花了一些时间才找到一个成功的替代方案。
content.en_text: He tried a number of different approaches and it took some time to find a successful alternative.

content.cn_text: 但最终他所做的是，他不再将每张照片制作在铜板上，而是制作在纸上，这在当时是一个巨大的进步。
content.en_text: But on the end what he did was instead of producing each photograph on a sheet of copper he produced them on paper and at the time this was a great advance.

content.cn_text: 塔尔博特称之为卡拉类型摄影的技术还有其他优点。
content.en_text: Talbot's technique which he called Cala type photography had other advantages as well.

content.cn_text: 也许最重要的是，照片本身不像以前制作的那么清晰。
content.en_text: Maybe the most important was that the pictures themselves weren't as sharp as the ones produced before.

content.cn_text: 清晰度一直是一个问题，因为它会扭曲一切，但这种新的卡拉类型工艺产生的图像与所有早期照片相比，更加逼真。
content.en_text: The sharpness had been a problem because it distorted everything but this new Cala type process produced images that were realistic compared to all earlier photographs.

content.cn_text: 卡拉类型摄影法在英国发明，但在法国摄影师中也很受欢迎。
content.en_text: Cala type photography was invented in Britain but it was also popular with French photographers.

content.cn_text: 19世纪50年代是法国快速发展的时期，摄影被用来记录这些变化。
content.en_text: The 1850s were a time of rapid development in France and photography was used to record these changes.

content.cn_text: 摄影变得多么重要的一个例子发生在1851年，当时法国政府成立了一个旨在促进摄影师利益的协会，这是以前从未发生过的事情。
content.en_text: One example of how important photography had become occurred in 1851 when the French government established a society to promote the interests of photographers, something that hadn't happened before.

content.cn_text: 最初有五名成员，其中也许最重要的一位是名叫古斯塔夫·勒格雷的人。
content.en_text: To begin with there were five members and maybe the most important was a man called Gustave Le Grey.

content.cn_text: 勒格雷对卡拉类型工艺越来越着迷。
content.en_text: Le Grey became increasingly fascinated by the Cala type process.

content.cn_text: 最重要的是，他想制作不同类型的图像，因此开始了一系列实验，看看如何实现。
content.en_text: Most of all he wanted to produce different types of image so began a series of experiments to see how this might be done.

content.cn_text: 他最大的成功是设计了第一台能够制作放大的相机，这是一个重要的进步。
content.en_text: His greatest success was when he designed the first camera that was able to create enlargements, an important step forward.

content.cn_text: 勒格雷的另一项创新是他对摄影所需设备尺寸的研究。
content.en_text: Another of Le Grey's innovations was his work on the size of the equipment necessary to take photographs.

content.cn_text: 最初的设备非常笨重，难以移动，但勒格雷使其更轻更小。
content.en_text: Originally it was very heavy and difficult to move about but Le Grey made it lighter and smaller.

content.cn_text: 这意味着他可以更容易地在户外拍照，而不必局限于工作室，而在此之前有时就是如此。
content.en_text: This meant he could take photographs outdoors more easily rather than being limited to the studio which had sometimes been the case until then.

content.cn_text: 在此期间，勒格雷创作了大量照片，并受到同行摄影师的高度评价。
content.en_text: Le Grey produced numerous photographs during this period and was highly regarded among fellow photographers.

content.cn_text: 但他的作品也在公众中广为人知，因为他写了一系列非常受欢迎的书籍，描述了卡拉类型工艺并展示了他的作品。
content.en_text: But his work also became famous among the general public because he wrote a very popular series of books describing the Cala type process and giving examples of his work.

content.cn_text: 事实很简单，人们喜欢摄影。他们认为它可靠，特别是与绘画相比，绘画中人手可能会改变或夸大事物。
content.en_text: The simple fact was that people liked photography. They thought it was reliable, especially compared to drawing where the human hand might alter or exaggerate things.

content.cn_text: 这带来了各种后果，其中之一是摄影现在经常被用来记录19世纪50年代法国历史建筑的大规模翻修工程。
content.en_text: This had various consequences, one of which was that photography was now often used to record the huge program of renovation work of historical buildings in France during the 1850s.

content.cn_text: 人们说，相机不会撒谎。
content.en_text: The camera doesn't lie, so people said.

content.cn_text: 因此，卡拉类型工艺不仅受到摄影师的赞赏，也越来越受到政府和主流社会的赞赏。
content.en_text: As a result, the Cala type process was admired not only by photographers but increasingly by the government and by mainstream society.

content.cn_text: 然而，关于摄影是否能真正被称为像雕塑或绘画一样的艺术形式，仍然存在争议。
content.en_text: However, debates still remained about whether photography could truly be called an art form like sculpture or drawing.

content.cn_text: 当然，尽管公众喜欢它，并且评论家们认为，摄影无法与其他既定的艺术形式相提并论。
content.en_text: Certainly, although the public liked it and the opinion of critics, photography could not be compared to other established forms of art.

content.cn_text: 那么，总而言之，我们能对这一时期的卡拉类型摄影说什么呢？
content.en_text: So, in summary, what can we say about Cala type photography during this period?

content.cn_text: 需要再次强调的是，19世纪50年代是英国和法国大发展的时期。
content.en_text: One thing to re-emphasise is that the 1850s were a period of great development in Britain and France.

content.cn_text: 两国文化都推崇所有新的技术进步，而摄影代表了这些思想，因为它被大多数人认为是科学。
content.en_text: The culture in both countries admired all new technological advances and photography represented these ideas because it was considered to be a science by most people.

content.cn_text: 然而，摄影师们并没有停止尝试新材料以提高作品质量。
content.en_text: However, photographers didn't stop experimenting with new materials to improve the quality of their work.

content.cn_text: 最重要的是，他们正在寻找使图像更持久的方法，因为卡拉类型照片会随着时间而褪色。
content.en_text: Most of all, they were searching for ways to make images more permanent because Cala type photographs tended to fade over time.

content.cn_text: 最后，他们发现如果图像制作在玻璃上，它会持续更长时间。
content.en_text: And in the end, they found that if the image was produced on glass, it lasted much longer.

content.cn_text: 因此，卡拉类型摄影逐渐被其他具有更大意义的技术所取代。
content.en_text: So, Cala type photography was slowly replaced by other techniques which had greater meaning.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。你现在有一分钟的时间来检查第四部分的答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of part four. You now have one minute to check your answers to part four.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: Daguerreotype had a problem: the use of copper made it 31. ______
Talbot was not originally a photographer but an artist who specialised in 32. ______
These calotype photographs were more 33. ______ because they weren’t so sharp.
He carried out experiments and produced the first 34. ______ using a camera.
He developed equipment that was easier to use outside the 35. ______
Calotype became famous because Le Gray produced 36. ______ about it.
It was used to record the work done on old 37. ______
Photography was thought of as a 39. ______, so reflected the culture of the 1850s.
The use of new materials, particularly 40. ______, led to new techniques.

Calotype photography in Britain and France
aFrom Daguerreotype to Calotype
• Daguerreotype was the first commercial photographic process.
• Daguerreotype had a problem: the use of copper made it $31$
• William Talbot began looking for an alternative approach in the 1840s.
• Talbot was not originally a photographer but an artist who specialised in $32$
• Talbot used paper instead of copper.
• These calotype photographs were more $33$ because they weren’t so sharp.
French Calotype
• In 1851, the first society for photographers was created.
• Gustave Le Gray became fascinated by calotype photography.
• He carried out experiments and produced the first $34$ using a camera.
• He developed equipment that was easier to use outside the $35$
• Calotype became famous because Le Gray produced $36$ about it.
• People thought photography was more reliable than drawing.
• It was used to record the work done on old $37$
• Photography was not ‘art’, according to $38$
Summary
• Photography was thought of as a $39$, so reflected the culture of the 1850s.
• The use of new materials, particularly $40$, led to new techniques.
